mnemonics.title: PICO - Parameterized Constructor Overview
mnemonics.content:
P - Parameters accept values
I - Initialization via arguments
C - Constructor called automatically on object creation
O - Overloading possible for multiple constructors

mnemonics.title: C.O.N.S.T - Constructor Naming Syntax
mnemonics.content:
C - Cannot have return type
O - Overloaded is allowed
N - Name same as class name
S - Same class, special member function
T - Types of parameters define uniqueness

mnemonics.title: E.I.L. - Explicit Initialization List
mnemonics.content:
E - Execute before body
I - Initialize members directly
L - List members in declaration order

acronyms.usage: MyClass(const MyClass& obj) - Creates copy of existing object
Constructor acquires resources, destructor releases them

visualTips.visualization: Draw flowchart: Object Declaration → Arguments Passed → Constructor Matching → Initialization List Executes → Constructor Body Executes → Object Ready
Show 3 boxes representing constructors with different parameter lists: () - default, (int) - single param, (int, int) - two params, all under same class name
Show class members vertically in declaration order, show initialization list arrows pointing to members - emphasize that order matters based on declaration, not list order

analogies.analogy: Like a factory that makes products with specific specifications - you provide parameters (color, size, material) and get a customized product.
Like photocopying a document - you already have one, and you create an exact duplicate.
Like directly filling form fields rather than writing and then erasing to write correct information.
Like a restaurant menu offering different meal combos - same restaurant (class), different options (constructors) to satisfy different needs.
